full_prompt:
Design the main wing spar for a Very Light Aeroplane (VLA) with MTOW 600 kg, wingspan 10.0 m, governed by EASA CS-VLA (Amendment 1, 2018). CS-VLA 337 specifies symmetric maneuver limit load factors n1 = +3.8 and n3 = -1.5. CS-VLA 303 specifies ultimate = 1.5 x limit. CS-VLA 341 specifies gust envelope with a derived gust velocity per Appendix. CS-VLA 625 adds a fitting factor of 1.15 on ultimate for primary fittings; this brief evaluates the spar itself, not the root fitting, so only the 1.5 factor applies.

Geometry. Rectangular-section wooden main spar (classic CS-VLA spruce) or composite alternative. This brief fixes a Sitka-spruce-laminate spar with plywood shear webs. Spar centerline at 30% chord. Root half-span 5.0 m. Wing chord tapers from 1.5 m at root to 1.1 m at tip. Spar caps: 80 mm wide x variable thickness 20-40 mm (design variable). Spar web: 2-ply birch plywood 6 mm each side. Ribs at 10 equal stations. Spar root attaches via a 3-bolt titanium end-fitting (out of scope for this brief).

Material. Sitka spruce per MIL-HDBK-1B: E_L = 11 GPa, rho = 400 kg/m^3, F_bending_L = 65 MPa, F_compression_L = 35 MPa (pass through grain), F_shear_perp_L = 7 MPa. Plywood web birch 6 mm plies: shear strength 10 MPa, E_in_plane = 8 GPa. No knockdown factor required for aircraft-grade spruce since airworthiness values are already minimum.

Service envelope. MTOW 600 kg * 9.81 = 5886 N. Wing loading W/S with S ~ 12 m^2 => 49 kg/m^2. CS-VLA 337: n1 = +3.8, n3 = -1.5 limit. CS-VLA 341 gust: derived gust velocity Ude at VC = 15.2 m/s (CS-VLA Appendix); compute gust-superposed load factor using Pratt formula with wing mass ratio mu_g. Typical result n_gust_VC = 3.2 (less than maneuver 3.8; maneuver governs for the spar). Root bending moment at n = +3.8: M_root_limit = n * W * eta * b/2 where eta is spanwise CP location (approx 0.4): M_root_limit = 3.8 * 5886 * 0.4 * 5.0 = 44,734 N*m. Root shear V_root_limit = 3.8 * 5886/2 = 11,184 N. Ultimate = 1.5 x limit => M_root_ult = 67,101 N*m, V_root_ult = 16,776 N.

Load cases. LC1 (positive maneuver ultimate n = +5.7): M_root = 67,101 N*m, V_root = 16,776 N. LC2 (negative maneuver ultimate n = -2.25): M_root = -26,487 N*m, V_root = -6,621 N. LC3 (positive gust ultimate n = 4.8 approximately): M_root = 56,500 N*m, V_root = 14,130 N. LC4 (landing condition per CS-VLA 473): vertical 3 g at main landing gear attach equivalent with 1.5 x factor, producing a local rear-spar moment 12,000 N*m. LC5 (wing-tip deflection at positive limit maneuver): max tip deflection at limit must be <= 12% of semispan = 0.6 m for VLA handling quality.

Pass/fail criteria. (R1) Spar-cap longitudinal stress at ultimate (LC1, LC3) must not exceed F_bending_L for spruce compression-side = 35 MPa (compression-critical) or F_bending_L tension-side = 65 MPa. (R2) Plywood web shear stress at ultimate must not exceed 10 MPa. (R3) Interlaminar / grain-perpendicular shear in spar caps at ultimate must not exceed 7 MPa. (R4) LC4 rear-spar local bending stress under landing must not exceed 35 MPa compression / 65 MPa tension. (R5) LC5 wing-tip deflection at limit (not ultimate) <= 0.6 m. (R6) Spar mass <= 9 kg per half-span (18 kg total). (R7) CS-VLA 471 (wheel-side landing) asymmetric case not evaluated here (out of scope for a spar design-for-pass); retained as out-of-scope note.

Solver and analysis expectations. Primary: composite-laminate FEM (shell + continuum shell) or Euler-Bernoulli beam model for caps + plate-bending shear web for plywood. Mesh 10 mm shells. Buckling of the compression cap is a production concern; for this brief treat local buckling via allowable-stress method (F_compression_L = 35 MPa already embeds the Eulerian reduction for typical spruce cap slenderness). Modal, thermal, fluid, radiation NOT required; buckling as a separate linear eigenvalue is NOT required.

Deliverables. STEP AP242 of spar structure, spar mass in kg, max spruce stress per LC (compression / tension / shear), web shear stress, wing-tip deflection at limit, and a pass/fail summary against CS-VLA 303/337/341.
short_prompt:
Design the main wing spar of a 600 kg MTOW Very Light Aeroplane (wingspan 10 m), traditionally a Sitka-spruce spar with birch-plywood shear webs. Design to EASA CS-VLA Amendment 1 (2018): 25.337-equivalent maneuver limits n1 = +3.8 and n3 = -1.5, ultimate = 1.5 x limit per CS-VLA 303, with gust envelope per CS-VLA 341 Appendix.

Envelope: rectangular spar at 30% chord, half-span 5.0 m, wing chord 1.5 m root to 1.1 m tip, spar caps 80 mm wide with 20-40 mm design-variable thickness, 6 mm 2-ply plywood webs, 10 equally spaced ribs, 3-bolt Ti end fitting (out of scope).

Load cases.
- LC1 positive maneuver ultimate (n = +5.7): root M = 67,101 N*m, V = 16,776 N.
- LC2 negative maneuver ultimate (n = -2.25): root M = -26,487 N*m, V = -6,621 N.
- LC3 positive gust ultimate (n ~ 4.8): root M = 56,500 N*m, V = 14,130 N.
- LC4 CS-VLA 473 landing equivalent: local rear-spar bending 12,000 N*m.
- LC5 wing-tip deflection at positive-limit maneuver <= 0.6 m (handling-quality proxy, 12% of semispan).

prompt.title: Spruce-plywood main spar for Very Light Aeroplane per CS-VLA
prompt.brief: 600 kg MTOW VLA main spar, n +3.8/-1.5 limit, ultimate 1.5x; spruce cap stress, plywood web shear, tip deflection <= 12% semispan.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: shell or beam for caps + plate for webs

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Flutter per CS-VLA 629 out of scope.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Compression-cap local buckling handled via spruce compression allowable.
